senses.definition: to become discouraged, often in the face of challenges

examples.x: She didn't lose heart despite failing the exam and decided to study harder.
It's important not to lose heart when facing difficulties; instead, stay determined to overcome them.
After several rejections, he started to lose heart in his job search.
They didn't lose heart even when the project encountered unexpected problems.
Losing heart in the middle of a marathon is common, but pushing through is essential to finish the race.

senses.definition: to reduce one's body mass or overall body weight

examples.x: She decided to adopt a healthier diet and exercise routine to lose weight.
His doctor recommended losing weight to improve his overall health and well-being.
He gradually lost weight through a combination of portion control and regular exercise.
Some individuals struggle to lose weight and seek professional guidance from dietitians or trainers.
Many people set New Year's resolutions to lose weight and maintain a healthy lifestyle.

word_list.pronunciation: lˈuːz ðə tɹˈʌst ʌv ˌɛsbˈiː slˈæʃ ˌɛstˌiːˈeɪtʃ

senses.definition: to no longer have someone believing in one's honest or reliability

examples.x: Breaking promises can lead to losing the trust of your colleagues.

word_list.pronunciation: lˈuːz wˈʌnz tɹˈʌst ɪn ˌɛsbˈiː slˈæʃ ˌɛstˌiːˈeɪtʃ

senses.definition: to no longer have confidence or faith in the honesty, reliability, or intentions of someone or something

examples.x: He has already lost his trust in the financial advisor who gave him poor investment advice.
After discovering that he had been dishonest about his finances, she lost her trust in him.
The employee lost her trust in her supervisor when he took credit for her ideas.

senses.definition: to temporarily or permanently be unable to speak or make vocal sounds due to a medical condition, strain, or illness

examples.x: She had to cancel her singing performance because she lost her voice.
After shouting at the sports event, he lost his voice for a few days.
The teacher's sore throat made her lose her voice and take a break from lecturing.
If you sing with a cold, you might risk losing your voice temporarily.
The singer had to undergo vocal rest after losing her voice from overexertion.
